metadata.description: Kungfu — the team's AI-agent skills marketplace: install a move, know the move. A shared Claude Code marketplace any project/agent can install from, plus the agent-rules discipline system (constitution + playbooks + SAFETY guard) and mirrored external skills.

plugins.name: wiki-doc-author
plugins.description: Produce the source docs an app feeds into the LLM Wiki (processor) — a compliant README for any kind (API / cronjob / worker / knowledge), plus a fresh openapi.json for OpenAPI-capable apps. Self-contained, bundled stdlib tools.

plugins.name: sop-to-spec
plugins.description: Convert an operations SOP (DBA runbook, infra procedure, deployment checklist) into a spec a human can approve and an AI agent can implement a three-layer FastAPI service from.

plugins.name: skill-author
plugins.description: Add or modify an installable Claude Code skill in this repo — produce a spec-compliant SKILL.md, bundle its scripts/references, and register it in the marketplace. Use when authoring a new skill or editing an existing one.

plugins.name: api-template-dev
plugins.description: Develop three-layer FastAPI services on the company API template — start a new service by cloning the template (architecture, devcontainer, lint/CI and built-in utilities like DB clients and mailers already in place), then add endpoints following the layered architecture, reusing built-in tools instead of reinventing them. Template specifics live in a regenerable facts layer with a rescan procedure.

plugins.name: agent-rules
plugins.description: Always-on constitution for weak models — a SessionStart hook injects 12 hard laws (evidence before claims, reproduce before fix, minimal diff, three-strike stop, no hallucinated APIs, tests are read-only, …) plus an end-of-turn checklist into every session. Ships situational rule files (DECISIONS / SAFETY / ANTIPATTERNS). Install alongside the dev-* playbook skills.

plugins.name: dev-bugfix
plugins.description: Fixed bug-fix procedure for any model tier — reproduce first, root-cause one-liner, minimal fix, machine-gated verification, fill-in report templates. Prevents 'should work now' fake completions.

plugins.name: dev-feature
plugins.description: Fixed feature-building procedure — acceptance checklist before code, copy existing patterns, smallest version, verify every checklist item with a command.

plugins.name: dev-refactor
plugins.description: Fixed refactor procedure — tests green before starting, one mechanical transform at a time with tests after each step, zero-behavior-change audit before reporting.

plugins.name: dev-investigate
plugins.description: Fixed investigation procedure — the deliverable is an answer with file:line evidence and a confidence label, never a code edit; found problems are reported, not fixed.

plugins.name: dev-review
plugins.description: Fixed code-review procedure — real diff only, correctness>security>failure-paths>contracts priority, every finding needs a concrete trigger, one line per finding, no praise padding, ends with a verdict.

plugins.name: dev-loop
plugins.description: End-to-end autonomous iteration loop for one coding requirement (loop engineering): lock intent once, establish machine gates, then act-observe-adjust until all gates are green and a PR is opened — no step-by-step human prompting; blocked work escalates via three-strike instead of thrashing.

plugins.name: dev-test
plugins.description: Fixed procedure for adding tests to existing code — behavior list first, one test at a time, and every test must prove it catches breakage (kill-proof: break the implementation, test must go red) so no evergreen fake tests survive.

plugins.name: agent-rules-setup
plugins.description: Runs this marketplace's skills-sync.sh for the user — installs the team skills and wires the agent-rules constitution + SAFETY guard into other agents (Codex/Gemini/Cline/OpenCode). The user never needs to know where the script lives; the skill locates it.

plugins.name: kungfu
plugins.description: Bundle — installs ALL of this repo's own skills at once (the full list is this entry's skills array; new skills added to it arrive automatically via marketplace auto-update).

plugins.name: superpowers
plugins.description: External — brainstorming, subagent-driven dev with code review, systematic debugging, red/green TDD, and skill authoring. Points at the public upstream; run localize.sh to swap it for an internal mirror. No sha = follows the default branch, so `/plugin marketplace update` pulls the latest.

plugins.name: andrej-karpathy-skills
plugins.description: External — behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes (Think Before Coding, Simplicity, …). Points at the public upstream; run localize.sh to swap it for an internal mirror. No sha = follows the default branch.
